title: A simplified precision dial: three heavy tick marks at the only declared state positions, a bold needle pointing to the current state. The simplification from 58-state-dial eliminates all subdivisions — there are no intermediate positions because Precept only declares what exists. The void arc between tick marks is not empty space but structural absence.
conceptualBridge: The three-tick dial means Precept's states are the only positions that exist because the definition declares exactly three — the dial has no intermediate marks because no intermediate states are declared, and the needle always reads exactly one.
decision: Kept: 4.06 avg — the simplified dial passes threshold; the instrument aesthetic is new and the three-tick + needle form is legible. The monochrome test weakness (3.5) suggests this needs either stronger monochrome differentiation between ticks and needle, or acceptance that the color is load-bearing.
